free_text: Measurement of cross sections producing short-lived
nuclei by 14 MeV neutrons - Br, Te, Dy, Ho, Yb
Two Ge detectors with efficiencies of 12 and
respectively, were used to measure the induced
activities. Each detector was covered with a 5 mm
acrylic absorber to reduce beta-rays. The efficiency
calibration was performed at a distance of 5 cm using
error of the efficiency curve is estimated to be 5, 3
and 1.5% in the energy region below 80 keV, 80 - 300
Samples were made from enriched and natural materials,
powder samples were wrapped in powder paper
10*10*0.2 mm. No further details are given.
The D-beam energy was 300 keV and the intensity
was 5 milliAmps. Another tube was put at -105 deg to
check the symmetry of the system. The irradiation
positions were about 15 cm from the tritium target.
Samples were placed on the angles 0, 50, 75, 105, 125
150 ang-deg relative to the d+ beam. A sample transport
system of six pneumatic tubes provided irradiation of
monochromatic neutrons of 13.4 - 14.9 MeV. The effec-
tive energy of incident neutrons at the irradiation
position was determined by the ratio of Zr-90(n,2n)/
Nb/Zr method. The error in neutron energy was estima-
The neutron flux at the irradiation positions was
determined with two aluminum monitor foils,
Experimental details and the details of the correction
The data were corrected for time fluctuation of the
neutron flux, contribution of scattered low-energy
neutrons, true coincidence sum, random coincidence
sum, thickness of samples, self absorption of gamma-
Total error equal to the quadratic sum of
several author names; ref. code corrected
